root_text.sn48.57:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā uruvelāyaṁ viharati najjā nerañjarāya tīre ajapālanigrodhe paṭhamābhisambuddho.
root_text.sn48.57:1.2: Atha kho bhagavato rahogatassa paṭisallīnassa evaṁ cetaso parivitakko udapādi:
root_text.sn48.57:1.3: “pañcindriyāni bhāvitāni bahulīkatāni amatogadhāni honti amataparāyaṇāni amatapariyosānāni.
root_text.sn48.57:1.5: Saddhindriyaṁ bhāvitaṁ bahulīkataṁ amatogadhaṁ hoti amataparāyaṇaṁ amatapariyosānaṁ.
root_text.sn48.57:1.9: paññindriyaṁ bhāvitaṁ bahulīkataṁ amatogadhaṁ hoti amataparāyaṇaṁ amatapariyosānaṁ.
root_text.sn48.57:1.10: Imāni pañcindriyāni bhāvitāni bahulīkatāni amatogadhāni honti amataparāyaṇāni amatapariyosānānī”ti.
root_text.sn48.57:2.1: Atha kho brahmā sahampati bhagavato cetasā cetoparivitakkamaññāya—seyyathāpi nāma balavā puriso samiñjitaṁ vā bāhaṁ pasāreyya, pasāritaṁ vā bāhaṁ samiñjeyya; evameva brahmaloke antarahito bhagavato purato pāturahosi.
root_text.sn48.57:2.2: Atha kho brahmā sahampati ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā yena bhagavā tenañjaliṁ paṇāmetvā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn48.57:2.4: Pañcindriyāni bhāvitāni bahulīkatāni amatogadhāni honti amataparāyaṇāni amatapariyosānāni.
root_text.sn48.57:2.6: Saddhindriyaṁ bhāvitaṁ bahulīkataṁ amatogadhaṁ hoti amataparāyaṇaṁ amatapariyosānaṁ …pe… paññindriyaṁ bhāvitaṁ bahulīkataṁ amatogadhaṁ hoti amataparāyaṇaṁ amatapariyosānaṁ.
root_text.sn48.57:2.7: Imāni pañcindriyāni bhāvitāni bahulīkatāni amatogadhāni honti amataparāyaṇāni amatapariyosānāni.
root_text.sn48.57:3.1: Bhūtapubbāhaṁ, bhante, kassape sammāsambuddhe brahmacariyaṁ acariṁ.
root_text.sn48.57:3.4: So khvāhaṁ, bhante, imesaṁyeva pañcannaṁ indriyānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā kāmesu kāmacchandaṁ virājetvā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ brahmalokaṁ upapanno.
root_text.sn48.57:3.8: Ahametaṁ jānāmi, ahametaṁ passāmi yathā imāni pañcindriyāni bhāvitāni bahulīkatāni amatogadhāni honti amataparāyaṇāni amatapariyosānānī”ti.

translation_text.sn48.57:1.1: At one time, when he was first awakened, the Buddha was staying in Uruvelā at the goatherd’s banyan tree on the bank of the Nerañjarā River.
translation_text.sn48.57:1.2: Then as he was in private retreat this thought came to his mind,
translation_text.sn48.57:1.3: “When these five faculties are developed and cultivated they have freedom from death as their objective, destination, and culmination.
translation_text.sn48.57:1.10: When these five faculties are developed and cultivated they have freedom from death as their objective, destination, and culmination.”
translation_text.sn48.57:2.1: Then the divinity Sahampati knew what the Buddha was thinking. As easily as a strong person would extend or contract their arm, he vanished from the realm of divinity and reappeared in front of the Buddha.
translation_text.sn48.57:2.2: He arranged his robe over one shoulder, raised his joined palms toward the Buddha, and said:
translation_text.sn48.57:2.3: “That’s so true, Blessed One! That’s so true, Holy One!
translation_text.sn48.57:2.4: When these five faculties are developed and cultivated they have freedom from death as their objective, destination, and culmination.
translation_text.sn48.57:2.6: The faculties of faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom.
translation_text.sn48.57:2.7: When these five faculties are developed and cultivated they have freedom from death as their objective, destination, and culmination.
translation_text.sn48.57:3.1: Once upon a time, sir, I lived the spiritual life under the fully awakened Buddha Kassapa.
translation_text.sn48.57:3.4: Because of developing and cultivating these same five faculties I lost desire for sensual pleasures. When my body broke up, after death, I was reborn in a good place, in the realm of divinity.
translation_text.sn48.57:3.7: That’s so true, Blessed One! That’s so true, Holy One!
translation_text.sn48.57:3.8: I know and see how when these five faculties are developed and cultivated they have freedom from death as their objective, destination, and culmination.”
